suggested_fixes: Add a TORCH_CHECK enforcing d >= 3584 && d <= 4608 if strict compliance with the stated runtime range is required.
Add checks that W, X, A, and B are on the same CUDA device.

If converting to a pure cuBLAS three-SGEMM implementation, include the required CUDA/cuBLAS headers, bind to the current PyTorch CUDA stream, avoid ATen matmul/addmm/mm, avoid explicit B.T materialization, and use the specified SGEMM parameter patterns.
warnings: Candidate is an ATen reference implementation and uses at::matmul for the core computation; this is acceptable only because it does not claim to be a pure cuBLAS three-SGEMM implementation.
Does not explicitly check that d is within the requested runtime range [3584, 4608], although the implementation is shape-general and not hardcoded.
Does not explicitly verify that all tensors are on the same CUDA device; ATen operations will typically raise an error if devices differ.
Materializes B.transpose(0, 1).contiguous(), which is correct but inefficient compared with using transpose operation flags in cuBLAS.
No explicit CUDA stream or kernel error checking is present, but this candidate does not launch custom CUDA kernels or call cuBLAS directly.
